deadline: April 23, 2026 at 3:00 p.m. (Round One) / October 8, 2026 at 3:00 p.m. (Round Two)
project_area: Emergency assistance for basic human needs (food, clothing, housing, utilities, transportation, child care, toiletries, prescription assistance)
demographic_requirements: Support for Rhode Islanders most in need, with priority given to volunteer-based organizations with minimal operational budgets
eligibility_summary: Nonprofit, 501(c)(3) organizations in Rhode Island providing direct assistance programs (e.g., food pantries, housing assistance) are eligible. Grants do not fund individuals, scholarships, capital/endowment efforts, fundraising events, or political campaigns. Established programs must have additional funding sources.
